board.title: Computer Architecture
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: This computer architecture puzzle explores the hardware, architectures, and design principles that shape modern processors. Processors have improved in performance since the Intel 4004, the first commercial microprocessor, was released in 1971.

clues.question: CPU design technique that increases throughput by overlapping stages of instruction execution; does not improve latency of individual instructions
Event from outside the CPU that disrupts instruction execution and transfers control to a handler; triggered by hardware, software, or timer events
Processing unit responsible for executing instructions; composed of an arithmetic logic unit, control unit, and registers
CPU architecture where instructions and data use the same memory and communication paths; can create a bottleneck when both compete for memory access
Family of instruction set architectures derived from the Intel 8086 processor; commonly used in desktops/servers and associated with CISC design
Storage within the CPU used to hold data during instruction execution; top of the memory hierarchy
Fast memory that sits between the CPU and RAM; stores recently or frequently accessed data to reduce RAM accesses
Memory management technique that maps virtual addresses to physical addresses using specialized address translation hardware (MMUs)
CPU architecture where instructions and data use physically separate memory; allows simultaneous instruction and data access
1965 observation that the number of transistors on an integrated circuit doubles about every two years with minimal increase in cost
Event generated by the CPU during instruction execution; forces the CPU to suspend execution, save state, and branch to a privileged handler
Instruction set design philosophy built around simple instructions; simplifies hardware design and pipelining
Family of instruction set architectures known for power efficiency; commonly used in mobile devices and associated with RISC design
Instruction set design philosophy built around complex instructions that can perform multiple operations
Shared communication system that transfers data, instructions, and control signals between hardware components
Volatile main memory that stores data and instructions for running programs; data can be accessed in near constant time regardless of location
